# AI Policy Compliance & Risk Analytics Engine

A data operations pipeline and interactive analysis dashboard designed to clean, tokenize, audit, and store raw unstructured AI legislation frameworks into a local relational database.

## System Architecture

- **`src/pipeline.py`**: Core backend data engine. Establishes SQLite structures, handles data ingestion, processes raw text into paragraph blocks, and executes word pattern matching to calculate compliance risk flag counts.
- **`src/dashboard.py`**: Frontend interface built on Streamlit. Connects to the SQLite storage layer via Pandas to display aggregate KPI metrics, data tables, and an interactive policy upload component.
- **`database/policy_data.db`**: Local SQLite relational database file tracking schemas.

## Database Schema

### Table: `compliance_documents`
- `doc_id` (INTEGER, PK, Auto-increment)
- `doc_title` (TEXT, Not Null)
- `jurisdiction` (TEXT, Not Null)
- `date_added` (TIMESTAMP, Default Current)

### Table: `document_chunks`
- `chunk_id` (INTEGER, PK, Auto-increment)
- `doc_id` (INTEGER, FK -> `compliance_documents.doc_id`)
- `chunk_index` (INTEGER, Not Null)
- `raw_text` (TEXT, Not Null)
- `risk_flag_count` (INTEGER, Default 0)

## Core Functions

### Ingestion Engine (`src/pipeline.py`)
- `get_db_connection()`: Connects to SQLite layout.
- `init_database()`: Enforces database table initialization structures.
- `evaluate_safety_tier(risk_count)`: Evaluates chunk density and matches validation labels ('PASS', 'NEEDS_REVIEW', 'FAIL').
- `ingest_policy_document(title, jurisdiction, full_text)`: Handles file array streams, strips white noise, evaluates chunks, and runs automatic transaction rollbacks on error states.

### Frontend Dashboard (`src/dashboard.py`)
- `load_dashboard_data()`: Uses Pandas `read_sql_query` to run analytical joins across both relational tables.
- `st.sidebar`: Hosts a transactional wrapper form using `st.form(key="ingestion_form")` to take runtime data inputs and call the backend injection engine without triggering broken UI re-render loops.

### Policy Diffusion Tracker (`src/tracker.py`)
- `load_regulatory_dataset()`: Initializes the policy indicators dataframe tracking sovereign variables (`Restriction_Score`, `Enforcement_Level`, `Enactment_Year`, `Oversight_Count`).
- Uses `st.multiselect` to apply vector column filtration across enforcement parameters and hot-reloads cross-sectional bar charts and legislative distribution grids on the main canvas.
